subject: maths
template: Write {p}% as a fraction out of 100.
explanation_template: {p}% means {p} out of 100, so it is {p}/100.

subject: maths
template: Write {p}% as a decimal.

subject: maths
template: Write {d} as a percentage.
explanation_template: {d} × 100 = {answer}%, so {d} = {answer}%.

subject: maths
template: Write {num}/{den} as a percentage.
explanation_template: {num}/{den} = {answer}%.

subject: maths
template: Find 10% of {amount}.
explanation_template: 10% of {amount} = {amount} ÷ 10 = {answer}.

subject: maths
template: Find 5% of {amount}.
explanation_template: 10% is {amount}/10. Half of that is 5%.

subject: maths
template: Find 1% of {amount}.
explanation_template: 1% of {amount} = {amount} ÷ 100 = {answer}.

subject: maths
template: Find {p}% of {amount}.
hint_template: Turn the percentage into a decimal first.

subject: maths
template: A jacket costs £{price}. It has {p}% off. What is the sale price?
hint_template: Find the discount, then subtract it.
explanation_template: The multiplier is 1 - {p}/100. Sale price = £{answer}.

subject: maths
template: Increase {amount} by {p}%.
explanation_template: Multiplier = 1 + {p}/100. {amount} × multiplier = {answer}.

subject: maths
template: Decrease {amount} by {p}%.
explanation_template: Multiplier = 1 - {p}/100. {amount} × multiplier = {answer}.

subject: maths
template: After a {p}% increase, a price is £{new_price}. What was the original price?
hint_template: The new price is more than 100% of the original.
explanation_template: Original = new price ÷ multiplier.

subject: maths
template: A toy is bought for £{cost} and sold for £{sale}. Work out the percentage profit.
hint_template: Profit percentage uses the original cost.
explanation_template: Profit = sale - cost. Percentage profit = profit ÷ cost × 100.

subject: maths
template: Shop A gives {a}% off £{priceA}. Shop B gives {b}% off £{priceB}. Which is cheaper?
explanation_template: Use price × (1 - discount/100) for each shop, then compare.

subject: maths
template: A value of {start} increases by {p}% for 2 years. What is the value after 2 years?
explanation_template: Compound increase means multiply by the same multiplier each year.

subject: maths
template: Simplify the ratio {a}:{b}.
hint_template: Divide both parts by the highest common factor.
explanation_template: Divide both sides by the same number to keep the comparison equal.

subject: maths
template: Complete the equivalent ratio: {a}:{b} = {ka}:__
explanation_template: Both parts of a ratio are multiplied by the same number.

subject: maths
template: A ratio is {a}:{b}. How many parts are there altogether?
explanation_template: Total parts = {a} + {b}.

subject: maths
template: Share £{total} in the ratio {a}:{b}. What is the larger share?
explanation_template: Add the parts, divide the total by total parts, then multiply.

subject: maths
template: Share {total} sweets in the ratio {a}:{b}:{c}. What does the middle person get?
hint_template: Add all parts, then find one part.
explanation_template: Middle share = {b} parts.

subject: maths
template: The ratio red:blue is {a}:{b}. There are {red} red counters. How many blue counters are there?
hint_template: Find the multiplier from red counters.
explanation_template: If {a} parts = {red}, then one part = {red}/{a}.

subject: maths
template: The ratio cats:dogs is {a}:{b}. There are {diff} more dogs than cats. How many dogs are there?
hint_template: The difference in parts matches the difference in animals.
explanation_template: {b}-{a} parts = {diff}, so find one part then dogs.

subject: maths
template: A recipe uses {a} cups of flour for {b} cups of sugar. How much sugar is needed for {flour} cups of flour?
hint_template: Scale both quantities by the same factor.
explanation_template: Find how many times bigger the flour amount is, then scale sugar.

subject: maths
template: {items} notebooks cost £{cost}. What is the cost of 1 notebook?
hint_template: Divide total cost by number of items.
explanation_template: Unit rate means cost per one item.

subject: maths
template: Pack A has {a_items} for £{a_cost}. Pack B has {b_items} for £{b_cost}. Which is better value?
explanation_template: Divide price by quantity for each pack. Lower unit price is better.

subject: maths
template: y is directly proportional to x. When x = {x1}, y = {y1}. Find y when x = {x2}.
explanation_template: Direct proportion means y = kx.

subject: maths
template: A map scale is 1 cm : {scale} km. Two towns are {cm} cm apart on the map. How far apart are they in real life?
explanation_template: Each centimetre represents {scale} km.

subject: maths
template: A class has boys:girls in the ratio {a}:{b}. What percentage of the class are girls?
hint_template: Girls are part of the total.
explanation_template: Total parts = {a}+{b}. Girl fraction = {b}/total.

subject: maths
template: Ali, Ben and Cara share money in the ratio {a}:{b}:{c}. Cara gets £{cara}. How much money is shared altogether?
hint_template: Use Cara to find one part.
explanation_template: Cara has {c} parts. Divide Cara amount by {c}, then multiply by total parts.

subject: maths
template: {workers} workers take {days} days to finish a job. How long would {new_workers} workers take at the same rate?
explanation_template: Total worker-days stays the same.

subject: maths
template: What are the coordinates of point A if it is {x} across and {y} up?
explanation_template: Go across first, then up/down.

subject: maths
template: Which point is at ({x}, {y})?
explanation_template: The first number tells horizontal position; second tells vertical.

subject: maths
template: A line goes up {rise} and across {run}. What is its gradient?
explanation_template: Gradient tells steepness: up divided by across.

subject: maths
template: A line goes down {fall} and across {run}. What is its gradient?
hint_template: A line going down has negative gradient.
explanation_template: Gradient is negative because the line slopes down left to right.

subject: maths
template: What is the y-intercept of y = {m}x + {c}?
hint_template: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept.
explanation_template: The line crosses the y-axis at c.

subject: maths
template: What is the gradient of y = {m}x + {c}?
hint_template: In y = mx + c, m is the gradient.
explanation_template: The coefficient of x is the gradient.

subject: maths
template: For y = {m}x + {c}, find y when x = {x}.
explanation_template: Replace x with {x}, then calculate.

subject: maths
template: A line has gradient {m} and y-intercept {c}. Write its equation.
explanation_template: Put the gradient in place of m and intercept in place of c.

subject: maths
template: A line is parallel to y = {m}x + {c}. What is its gradient?
hint_template: Parallel lines have the same gradient.
explanation_template: Same steepness means same gradient.

subject: maths
template: Find the gradient between ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2}).
hint_template: Gradient = change in y ÷ change in x.
explanation_template: Find rise over run between the two points.

subject: maths
template: On a distance-time graph, the distance at {time} minutes is {dist} m. How far has the person travelled?
explanation_template: Distance is read from the y-axis.

subject: maths
template: On a distance-time graph, a horizontal line lasts from {t1} to {t2} minutes. How long was the stop?
hint_template: Horizontal means no change in distance.
explanation_template: Stop time is the difference between times.

subject: maths
template: A cyclist travels {dist} km in {time} hours. Find the average speed in km/h.
explanation_template: Divide kilometres by hours.

subject: maths
template: A runner goes {dist} km from home in {out_time} minutes, rests for {rest} minutes, then returns in {back_time} minutes. How long is the whole journey?
explanation_template: Whole journey includes outward time, rest time and return time.

subject: maths
template: A taxi fare is y = {m}x + {c}, where x is miles. What is the fare for {x} miles?
explanation_template: Calculate fixed charge plus cost per mile.

subject: maths
template: Which probability means impossible?
explanation_template: The probability scale runs from 0 to 1.

subject: maths
template: A bag has {red} red counters and {blue} blue counters. What is P(red)?
explanation_template: Add counters, then put red over total.

subject: maths
template: The probability of rain is {p}. What is the probability it does not rain?
hint_template: Probabilities for all outcomes add to 1.
explanation_template: Not rain = 1 - rain probability.

subject: maths
template: The probability of winning is {num}/{den}. If {trials} people play, how many are expected to win?
hint_template: Expected frequency = probability × number of trials.
explanation_template: Multiply the probability by the number of people.

subject: maths
template: A spinner has {total} equal sections and {win} are green. What is P(green)?
explanation_template: All sections are equally likely.

subject: maths
template: A fair dice is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a number greater than {n}?
explanation_template: Numbers greater than {n} are counted out of 6.

subject: maths
template: A card is chosen from numbers 1 to {n}. What is the probability of choosing an even number?
explanation_template: Even outcomes divided by total outcomes.

subject: maths
template: Two fair coins are flipped. What is the probability of getting two heads?
explanation_template: HH, HT, TH, TT are equally likely.

subject: maths
template: A probability table has P(red)={r}, P(blue)={b}. The only other colour is green. Find P(green).
explanation_template: Subtract known probabilities from 1.

subject: maths
template: A spinner lands on blue {blue} times in {trials} spins. Estimate P(blue).
explanation_template: Use results from the experiment.

subject: maths
template: In a class, {girls} girls and {boys} boys choose art. There are {total} students. What is the probability a randomly chosen student chose art?
hint_template: Total choosing art over class total.
explanation_template: Add girls and boys who chose art.

subject: maths
template: A group has {total} students. {a} are boys. {pass_boys} boys passed a test and {pass_girls} girls passed. How many students passed?
hint_template: Add passed boys and passed girls.
explanation_template: Frequency trees split totals into branches.

subject: maths
template: A bag has {red} red and {blue} blue counters. One counter is replaced after choosing. What is P(red then red)?
hint_template: With replacement, probabilities stay the same.
explanation_template: Multiply P(red) by P(red).

subject: maths
template: A coin is flipped and a dice is rolled. What is the probability of heads and a 6?
explanation_template: P(heads)=1/2 and P(6)=1/6.

subject: maths
template: A probability is written as {bad}. Why is this impossible?
explanation_template: No probability can be less than 0 or greater than 1.

subject: maths
template: Round {n} to the nearest 10.
explanation_template: 0-4 rounds down; 5-9 rounds up.

subject: maths
template: Round {n} to the nearest 100.
explanation_template: If the tens digit is 5 or more, round up.

subject: maths
template: Write one factor of {n} greater than 1.
explanation_template: Factors divide a number with no remainder.

subject: maths
template: Write the first three multiples of {n}.
explanation_template: Multiply by 1, 2 and 3.

subject: maths
template: Find the HCF of {a} and {b}.
explanation_template: The HCF is the highest common factor.

subject: maths
template: Find the LCM of {a} and {b}.
explanation_template: The LCM is the lowest common multiple.

subject: maths
template: Is {n} prime?
explanation_template: A prime number has only 1 and itself as factors.

subject: maths
template: What is {n} squared?

subject: maths
template: What is {n} cubed?
hint_template: Cubed means multiply by itself three times.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a} + ({b}).
explanation_template: Move right for positive, left for negative.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a} - ({b}).
explanation_template: Careful with double negatives.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a} + {b} × {c}.
explanation_template: Use BIDMAS order.

subject: maths
template: Estimate {a} × {b} by rounding both numbers to 1 significant figure.
explanation_template: Estimation gives an approximate answer.

subject: maths
template: Write {n} as a product of prime factors.
explanation_template: Keep dividing by primes until only primes remain.

subject: maths
template: A bus leaves every {a} minutes and a train leaves every {b} minutes. They both leave at 09:00. After how many minutes will they next leave together?
explanation_template: Find the lowest common multiple of the two times.

subject: maths
template: Complete: {a}/{b} = {ka}/__
hint_template: Multiply numerator and denominator by same number.
explanation_template: Equivalent fractions have the same value.

subject: maths
template: Simplify {a}/{b}.
hint_template: Divide top and bottom by the same factor.
explanation_template: Use the highest common factor if you can.

subject: maths
template: Which is bigger: {f1} or {f2}?
explanation_template: Convert both fractions to comparable forms.

subject: maths
template: Put these in ascending order: {f1}, {f2}, {f3}.
explanation_template: Use common denominators.

subject: maths
template: Find {num}/{den} of {amount}.
hint_template: Divide by denominator, multiply by numerator.
explanation_template: Find one part first.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a}/{d} + {b}/{d}.
explanation_template: Keep the denominator the same.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a}/{d} - {b}/{d}.
explanation_template: Keep the denominator the same.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {f1} + {f2}.
explanation_template: Rewrite both fractions with the same denominator.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a}/{b} × {c}/{d}.
explanation_template: Numerator × numerator, denominator × denominator.

subject: maths
template: Calculate {a}/{b} ÷ {c}/{d}.
explanation_template: Dividing by a fraction means multiplying by its reciprocal.

subject: maths
template: Write {whole} {num}/{den} as an improper fraction.
hint_template: Multiply whole by denominator, add numerator.
explanation_template: Keep the denominator the same.

subject: maths
template: Write {num}/{den} as a decimal.
explanation_template: Fraction bar means divide.

subject: maths
template: Write {num}/{den} as a percentage.
hint_template: Convert to a decimal then percentage.
explanation_template: Multiply the decimal by 100.

subject: maths
template: A club has {total} members. {num}/{den} are girls. How many boys are there?
hint_template: Find girls first, then subtract from total.
explanation_template: Boys are the remaining members.

subject: maths
template: A tank is {num}/{den} full. It holds {capacity} litres when full. How many more litres are needed to fill it?
explanation_template: Amount needed = remaining fraction of capacity.

subject: maths
template: Find x if x and {a}° are on a straight line.
hint_template: Angles on a straight line add to 180°.

subject: maths
template: Angles around a point include {a}°, {b}° and x. Find x.
hint_template: Angles around a point add to 360°.
explanation_template: Subtract known angles from 360.

subject: maths
template: Two vertically opposite angles are {a}° and x. Find x.
explanation_template: Opposite angles made by crossing lines are equal.

subject: maths
template: A triangle has angles {a}°, {b}° and x. Find x.
hint_template: Angles in a triangle add to 180°.
explanation_template: Subtract the two known angles from 180.

subject: maths
template: A quadrilateral has angles {a}°, {b}°, {c}° and x. Find x.
hint_template: Angles in a quadrilateral add to 360°.
explanation_template: Subtract known angles from 360.

subject: maths
template: In parallel lines, a corresponding angle to {a}° is x. Find x.
explanation_template: Same relative position means same size.

subject: maths
template: In parallel lines, an alternate angle to {a}° is x. Find x.
explanation_template: Z-shaped angles are equal.

subject: maths
template: Co-interior angles are {a}° and x. Find x.
explanation_template: C-shaped angles in parallel lines sum to 180.

subject: maths
template: An isosceles triangle has vertex angle {a}°. Find each base angle.
hint_template: Base angles in an isosceles triangle are equal.
explanation_template: Subtract the vertex angle, then divide by 2.

subject: maths
template: What is each angle in an equilateral triangle?

subject: maths
template: A triangle has inside angles {a}° and {b}°. Find the exterior angle at the third vertex.
hint_template: Exterior angle equals sum of opposite interior angles.
explanation_template: You can also find third angle then subtract from 180.

subject: maths
template: Find the sum of interior angles of a {n}-sided polygon.
explanation_template: Split the polygon into triangles.

subject: maths
template: Find each interior angle of a regular {n}-gon.
hint_template: Find total then divide by number of sides.
explanation_template: Regular means all angles are equal.

subject: maths
template: A straight line contains angles {a}°, {b}° and x. Find x and give the reason.
hint_template: Angles on a straight line add to 180°.
explanation_template: Subtract both known angles from 180.

subject: maths
template: Angles on a straight line are {a}x and {b}x. Find x.

subject: maths
template: Find the area of a rectangle with length {l} cm and width {w} cm.
explanation_template: Multiply the two side lengths.

subject: maths
template: Find the perimeter of a rectangle with length {l} cm and width {w} cm.
explanation_template: Perimeter = 2l + 2w.

subject: maths
template: Find the area of a triangle with base {b} cm and height {h} cm.
explanation_template: It is half a rectangle.

subject: maths
template: Find the area of a parallelogram with base {b} cm and height {h} cm.
explanation_template: Use vertical height, not slanted side.

subject: maths
template: A compound shape is made from rectangles with areas {a} cm² and {b} cm². Find the total area.
explanation_template: Compound area can be found by splitting into rectangles.

subject: maths
template: A rectangle has area {area} cm² and width {w} cm. Find its length.
explanation_template: Divide area by known side.

subject: maths
template: A rectangle has perimeter {p} cm and length {l} cm. Find its width.
hint_template: Half the perimeter is length + width.
explanation_template: Perimeter = 2(l+w).

subject: maths
template: Find the area of a trapezium with parallel sides {a} cm and {b} cm, height {h} cm.
hint_template: Average the parallel sides, then multiply by height.
explanation_template: Formula: 1/2(a+b)h.

subject: maths
template: Find the area of a circle with radius {r} cm. Use π = 3.14.
explanation_template: Square the radius, then multiply by π.

subject: maths
template: Find the circumference of a circle with diameter {d} cm. Use π = 3.14.
explanation_template: Multiply diameter by π.

subject: maths
template: A garden is {l} m by {w} m. Turf costs £{cost} per m². Find the total cost.
hint_template: Find area first, then multiply by cost.
explanation_template: This is a two-step area problem.

subject: maths
template: A rectangular field is {l} m by {w} m. Fencing costs £{cost} per metre. Find the cost to fence it.
explanation_template: Cost = perimeter × cost per metre.

subject: maths
template: A {big} cm by {big} cm square has a {small} cm by {small} cm square removed. Find the remaining area.
explanation_template: Shaded area = large area - removed area.

subject: maths
template: A rectangle has area {area} cm². It is enlarged by scale factor {sf}. What is the new area?
hint_template: Area scale factor is square of length scale factor.
explanation_template: Multiply area by {sf}².

subject: maths
template: A rectangle has area {area} cm² and one side {w} cm. Find its perimeter.
hint_template: Find missing length from area first.
explanation_template: Length = area ÷ width, then find perimeter.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: x^{a} × x^{b}.
hint_template: When multiplying same base, add powers.
explanation_template: Same base means add indices.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: x^{a} ÷ x^{b}.
hint_template: When dividing same base, subtract powers.
explanation_template: Subtract the lower power from the upper power.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: (x^{a})^{b}.
hint_template: Power of a power means multiply powers.
explanation_template: Multiply the indices together.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: {c}x^{a} × {d}x^{b}.
explanation_template: Handle numbers and powers separately.

subject: maths
template: What is {a}^0?
hint_template: Any non-zero number to power 0 is 1.
explanation_template: This is a key index law.

subject: maths
template: Write x^{- {a}} using a positive index.
explanation_template: Move it to the denominator.

subject: maths
template: Write {n} in standard form.
hint_template: Move decimal after first non-zero digit.
explanation_template: Standard form is a × 10^n where 1 ≤ a < 10.

subject: maths
template: Write {n} in standard form.
hint_template: Small numbers have negative powers of 10.
explanation_template: Move decimal to make a number between 1 and 10.

subject: maths
template: Write {a} × 10^{p} as an ordinary number.
hint_template: Move decimal right for positive powers.
explanation_template: 10^p means multiply by 10 p times.

subject: maths
template: Which is larger: {a}×10^{p} or {b}×10^{q}?
explanation_template: If powers match, compare the front number.

subject: maths
template: Calculate ({a}×10^{p}) × ({b}×10^{q}) in standard form.
hint_template: Multiply front numbers and add powers.
explanation_template: Adjust if the front number is 10 or more.

subject: maths
template: Calculate ({a}×10^{p}) ÷ ({b}×10^{q}) in standard form.
hint_template: Divide front numbers and subtract powers.
explanation_template: Use index laws for powers of 10.

subject: maths
template: A cell is {a}×10^{- {p}} m wide. Write this as a decimal.
explanation_template: Convert from standard form carefully.

subject: maths
template: Solve: 2^x = {n}.
explanation_template: Find which power gives {n}.

subject: maths
template: Simplify fully: (x^{a} × x^{b}) ÷ x^{c}.
explanation_template: Apply index laws in order.

subject: maths
template: Find the volume of a cuboid {l} cm by {w} cm by {h} cm.
explanation_template: Multiply the three dimensions.

subject: maths
template: Find the volume of a cube with side {s} cm.
explanation_template: Multiply side by itself three times.

subject: maths
template: Find the surface area of a cube with side {s} cm.
explanation_template: Area of one face is s², then multiply by 6.

subject: maths
template: Find the surface area of a cuboid {l} by {w} by {h}.
explanation_template: Surface area = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh.

subject: maths
template: A prism has cross-section area {area} cm² and length {length} cm. Find its volume.
hint_template: Volume of prism = cross-section area × length.
explanation_template: Multiply the area by the length.

subject: maths
template: Find the volume of a cylinder radius {r} cm and height {h} cm. Use π=3.14.
explanation_template: Find circle area, then multiply by height.

subject: maths
template: A cuboid has volume {v} cm³, length {l} cm and width {w} cm. Find its height.
explanation_template: Divide volume by base area.

subject: maths
template: A solid is made from cuboids with volumes {v1} cm³ and {v2} cm³. Find total volume.
explanation_template: Compound volume can be split into simpler solids.

subject: maths
template: How many cm³ are in {n} m³?
hint_template: 1 m = 100 cm, so cube the scale factor.

subject: maths
template: A block has mass {m} g and volume {v} cm³. Find its density.
explanation_template: Divide grams by cubic centimetres.

subject: maths
template: A cube of side {s} cm is painted on all faces. Paint costs £{cost} per cm². Find the total cost.
explanation_template: Cost = surface area × cost per cm².

subject: maths
template: A tank is {l} cm by {w} cm by {h} cm. How many litres can it hold?
explanation_template: Find volume in cm³, then divide by 1000.

subject: maths
template: A solid has volume {v} cm³. It is enlarged by scale factor {sf}. What is the new volume?
hint_template: Volume scale factor is cube of length scale factor.
explanation_template: Multiply by scale factor cubed.

subject: maths
template: A solid has surface area {sa} cm². It is enlarged by scale factor {sf}. What is the new surface area?
hint_template: Area scale factor is square of length scale factor.
explanation_template: Multiply by scale factor squared.

subject: maths
template: A cone has radius {r} cm and height {h} cm. Use π=3.14. Find its volume to 1 dp.
hint_template: Cone volume is one third of cylinder.
explanation_template: Use 1/3 πr²h.

subject: maths
template: In a right-angled triangle, what is the hypotenuse?
hint_template: It is opposite the right angle.
explanation_template: The hypotenuse is always the longest side.

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has short sides {a} cm and {b} cm. Find the hypotenuse.
explanation_template: Square the two shorter sides, add, then square root.

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has hypotenuse {c} cm and one short side {a} cm. Find the other short side.
explanation_template: Subtract squares, then square root.

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has sides {a} cm and {b} cm. Find the hypotenuse to 1 decimal place.
explanation_template: c² = a² + b².

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has hypotenuse {c} cm and one side {a} cm. Find the missing side to 1 decimal place.
explanation_template: Missing short side uses hypotenuse squared minus known side squared.

subject: maths
template: A ladder reaches {h} m up a wall and its foot is {d} m from the wall. How long is the ladder?
explanation_template: Wall and ground make a right angle.

subject: maths
template: A rectangle is {l} cm by {w} cm. Find the diagonal to 1 decimal place.
explanation_template: Split the rectangle into right triangles.

subject: maths
template: Find the distance between ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2}).
explanation_template: The points form a right triangle.

subject: maths
template: Is a triangle with sides {a}, {b}, {c} right-angled?
explanation_template: If shorter squares add to longest square, it is right-angled.

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has legs {a} cm and {b} cm. Find its perimeter.
explanation_template: Perimeter is all three sides added.

subject: maths
template: A cuboid is {l} by {w} by {h}. Find the base diagonal to 1 dp.
hint_template: Base diagonal uses length and width only.
explanation_template: This is Pythagoras on the base rectangle.

subject: maths
template: A wire of length {c} m reaches from the top of a pole to a point {d} m from its base. Find the pole height.
explanation_template: Height² = wire² - ground distance².

subject: maths
template: Route A is {a} m then {b} m at right angles. Route B is the direct diagonal. How much shorter is route B to 1 dp?
explanation_template: Route A is two sides; Route B is hypotenuse.

subject: maths
template: Find the diagonal of a square with side {s} cm to 1 decimal place.
explanation_template: Diagonal² = s² + s².

subject: maths
template: A right triangle has hypotenuse {c} cm and one side {a} cm. Find its area.
explanation_template: Area = 1/2 × two perpendicular sides.

subject: maths
template: Solve: x + y = {s}, x - y = {d}. Find x.
explanation_template: Adding eliminates y.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {a}x + y = {s1}, {b}x + y = {s2}. Find x.
explanation_template: Same y coefficient means subtraction removes y.

subject: maths
template: Solve: x + {a}y = {s1}, x + {b}y = {s2}. Find y.
explanation_template: Same x coefficient means subtraction removes x.

subject: maths
template: Solve: x + y = {s}, {a}x + y = {t}. Find x.
explanation_template: The y terms match already.

subject: maths
template: Solve: y = {m}x + {c}, x + y = {s}. Find x.
hint_template: Substitute y into the second equation.
explanation_template: Replace y with the expression in x.

subject: maths
template: Solve: x + y = {s}, x - y = {d}. Find y.
explanation_template: Subtract or add carefully with negatives.

subject: maths
template: A pen and pencil cost £{s}. A pen costs £{d} more than a pencil. Find the cost of the pen.
hint_template: Let p be pen and q be pencil.
explanation_template: Use p+q=total and p-q=difference.

subject: maths
template: 2 adult tickets and 1 child ticket cost £{t1}. 1 adult and 1 child ticket cost £{t2}. Find the adult ticket price.
explanation_template: The difference gives one adult ticket.

subject: maths
template: Two angles add to {s}°. One is {d}° larger than the other. Find the larger angle.
explanation_template: This is simultaneous reasoning.

subject: maths
template: Plan A costs £{fixedA}+£{rateA} per lesson. Plan B costs £{fixedB}+£{rateB} per lesson. After how many lessons are costs equal?
explanation_template: Solve fixedA + rateA x = fixedB + rateB x.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {a}x + y = {s1}, {b}x + {c}y = {s2}. Find x.
explanation_template: You may need to multiply an equation first.

subject: maths
template: The lines y = {m1}x + {c1} and y = {m2}x + {c2} intersect. Find x.
hint_template: At intersection, y values are equal.
explanation_template: Set the two expressions equal and solve.

subject: maths
template: Solve the pair: {a}x = {b}, y = x + {c}. Find y.
explanation_template: This uses substitution after solving x.

subject: maths
template: A number x and number y add to {s}. Also, 2x + y = {t}. Find x.
hint_template: Subtract the first equation from the second.
explanation_template: 2x+y minus x+y leaves x.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {a}x + {b}y = {s1}, {c}x - {d}y = {s2}. Find x.
explanation_template: Multiply equations if needed, then add or subtract.

subject: maths
template: Bananas cost {b_cost}p each and apples cost {a_cost}p each. Write an expression for b bananas and a apples.
hint_template: Cost per item times number of items.
explanation_template: Total cost = banana cost + apple cost.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: {a}x + {b}x.
explanation_template: Add the coefficients, keep x.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: {a}x + {c} + {b}x + {d}.
hint_template: Collect x terms and constants separately.
explanation_template: Like terms have the same letter part.

subject: maths
template: Simplify: {a}x + {b}y + {c}x + {d}y.
hint_template: Group x terms and y terms.
explanation_template: Only like terms combine.

subject: maths
template: Find {m}a + {c} when a = {a}.
explanation_template: Substitution means putting the number in place of the letter.

subject: maths
template: Find {m}a + {c} when a = {a}.
explanation_template: Replace a first, then calculate.

subject: maths
template: Expand: {m}(x + {a}).
hint_template: Multiply both terms inside the bracket.
explanation_template: Every term inside the bracket is multiplied by {m}.

subject: maths
template: Expand: -{m}(x + {a}).
hint_template: Multiply both terms by the negative number.
explanation_template: The negative changes both signs.

subject: maths
template: Expand and simplify: {a}(x + {b}) + {c}(x + {d}).
explanation_template: Then collect like terms.

subject: maths
template: Solve: x + {a} = {b}.
explanation_template: Subtract {a} from both sides.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {m}x = {b}.
explanation_template: Divide both sides by {m}.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {m}x + {a} = {b}.
explanation_template: Use inverse operations in reverse order.

subject: maths
template: Solve: {a}x + {b} = {c}x + {d}.
hint_template: Collect x terms on one side.
explanation_template: Subtract the smaller x term from both sides first.

subject: maths
template: Factorise fully: {a}x + {b}.
explanation_template: Take the largest common factor outside the bracket.

subject: maths
template: Factorise: x² + {sum}x + {prod}.
hint_template: Find two numbers that add and multiply correctly.
explanation_template: The two bracket numbers add to the x coefficient and multiply to the constant.
